summary: Large gm_1832 decompilation expansion covering Classic/1P intro splash behavior, VS intro camera/model scene setup, and Training Mode HUD/menu state. The PR replaced many stubs in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c with C, added typed game-mode structures and prototypes, and improved decomp.dev matched code by +1932 bytes (+0.05%) with 5 new 100% matches and 30 fuzzy improvements. Evidence also notes one slight broken match, fn_801884F8, attributed by the author to data/offset issues expected to resolve when linking.

key_files.role: Main change: ~2131 added lines replacing gm_1832 stubs with Classic intro, VS intro, and Training Mode implementations plus local structs, static data, bitfields, pragmas, and stack padding.
Updated many gm_1832 prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures such as fn_8018504C(void), fn_80185A0C(void), fn_80187910(HSD_GObj*), gm_80189CDC(StartMeleeData*), and fn_8018A000(void).
Added CssSubStruct and TrainingModeState, turning raw Training Mode/BSS indexing into typed fields for players, result_cache, jobjs, anim_frames, menu_values, and text.
Refined fn_80160DE8 signature to void fn_80160DE8(HSD_JObj*, u8, s32, u8, f32, f32), enabling typed calls from gm_1832 splash/name rendering code.

what_changed: Implemented or substantially filled many gm_1832 functions previously represented by stub comments, with decomp.dev reporting 5 new 100% matches: fn_801861B8, fn_8018A364, fn_80188D3C, fn_8018504C, and gm_80186E30_OnEnter.

Added Classic/1P intro and splash-screen logic: character model positioning in fn_8018325C/fn_80184138/fn_80186F6C, splash timer/text/audio behavior in fn_80184AB8/fn_8018504C, character setup in fn_801851C0/fn_80185E34/fn_80185F5C/fn_80186080, SObj/camera setup in fn_801857C4/fn_80185A0C/fn_801861B8/fn_80186634, and enter-data copy in gm_80186E30_OnEnter.

Added VS intro scene/camera work around lbl_804736C0: fn_80187910 camera animation scaling, fn_80187AB4 and fn_80187CF4 animation state machines, and gm_80187F48_OnEnter archive/camera/light/model/audio setup using static data including lbl_804D4138 = "IrNml" and lbl_803D9750.

Added Training Mode state/HUD/menu code: fn_801884F8 result caching, fn_80188550 player-count adjustment, fn_80188644 state reset, fn_80188738/fn_80188910/fn_80188B3C/fn_80188D3C digit animation helpers, fn_80188EE8 HUD update, fn_801891F4 menu handler, fn_80189B88 setup, gm_80189CDC initialization, and fn_8018A000 text/archive setup for "GmTrain".

Exposed and documented hidden struct layout in globals such as lbl_804735A8, lbl_804735E8, lbl_804736C0, and lbl_80473700; added local layout structs ClassicProcArray, ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicCharData, ClassicModeEnterData, CssSubStruct, and TrainingModeState.

Updated includes for needed baselib and melee systems: aobj/cobj/dobj/mobj/wobj/gobjplink/sislib/random/runtime, gm_1601/gm_1A36/gmmain_lib, ifstatus/ifall, it_266F, lb_0192, lblanguage, mn/types, and mnname.

The decomp.dev report also recorded 30 improvements in unmatched gm_1832 items and one broken match: fn_801884F8 went from 100.00% to 99.82%; the author said this was "data / offset stuff" expected to be resolved when linking.
smart_moves: Converted raw padding and pointer arithmetic into offset-annotated structs with STATIC_ASSERTs for critical table layouts, making large archive/BSS data accesses readable without pretending every field is semantically known.

Used native C bitfields for lbl_804736C0.x36 and lbl_804736C0.x37 to express stage_index/done/flash/active and animation state bytes; commit metadata explicitly notes this helped bitfield/rlwimi codegen and made gm_8018838C_OnFrame 100%.

Controlled MWCC behavior with localized #pragma dont_inline, #pragma push/pop, inline alternatives, and noInline wrapper calls where inlining/scheduling affected matches, rather than globally destabilizing the unit.
Kept remaining unknowns honest: address-based function/global names stayed in place, while prototypes and struct fields were only refined where evidence from calls/layout was available.

Accepted useful high-percentage fuzzy matches when the remaining differences were documented as systemic BSS/data/SDA offsets, register swaps, or scheduling/prologue issues; this moved the unit forward instead of overfitting source to relocation noise.
Pulled shared state into TrainingModeState and CssSubStruct so code such as result_cache, players, jobjs, anim_frames, menu_values, and text accesses replaced ptr[index] style BSS indexing.
Used typed HSD_JObj/HSD_GObj/HSD_CObj/SisLib/archive APIs and added the exact headers needed, which improved prototypes and reduced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS spread.

Final commit metadata mentions cleanup of pointer arithmetic, fixing build issues, removing problematic inlining, and fixing an out-of-bounds read; those are credible cleanup signals, though the slice does not show reviewer discussion for them.
decomp_lessons: For large game-mode files, a broad typed-structure pass can produce many near-matches even when only a few functions reach 100%; documenting systemic relocation/register differences makes those fuzzy matches reviewable.
When a BSS/global blob is blocking progress, introduce nested structs around observed offsets and keep offset-style member names plus padding until semantics are proven.
Use STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...)) on newly inferred layout structs such as ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, and ClassicSplashRow to protect archive/table offset assumptions.
For state bytes that the original manipulates with bit operations, C bitfields can be a better match than manual shifts/masks if field order and widths are modeled carefully.
Stack shape still matters in this unit: PAD_STACK sizes were used repeatedly, and commit metadata calls out a PAD_STACK adjustment for fn_80186F6C as a match improvement.
Locale/text rendering code may require byte arrays and explicit SisLib calls instead of normal C string assumptions; this PR used raw byte arrays for Japanese/US text fragments and language checks.
Training/HUD digit rendering patterns repeat: gather sibling JObjs, clamp values to 999, request digit animations with 10.0f as the blank frame, then animate all three nodes.

Archive-driven scene setup often benefits from preserving duplicated switch-case structure and call order, even when the C could be factored, because MWCC scheduling and data relocations are sensitive.
If a function regresses only because typed data moved offsets, note it explicitly and check whether the linker/data layout will resolve it before undoing useful source cleanup.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-style names such as fn_8018325C, fn_801884F8, and fn_8018A364; game-mode lifecycle functions retained gm_8018XXXX_OnEnter/OnFrame/OnLeave naming.

Newly inferred structs used descriptive PascalCase where the domain was clear: ClassicProcArray, ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicCharData, ClassicModeEnterData, CssSubStruct, and TrainingModeState.

Struct fields stayed offset-based when semantics were incomplete, e.g. x00, x04, x6A8, pad_570, xF1, xF4, and x37, with comments added where evidence was strong such as left/right char ids, costumes, metal flags, and splash SObj display GObj.
Global/static symbols preserved lbl_803*/lbl_804* address naming, including lbl_804735A8, lbl_804735E8, lbl_804736C0, lbl_80473700, lbl_803D9750, and lbl_804D4138.
Object locals followed common decomp shorthand: gobj, jobj, cobj, sub, desc, anim_idx, arg0_ for untyped/raw input before casting.
Header cleanup convention: replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with the narrowest evidenced type signature, but use void* or s32 rather than inventing semantic enum names when the exact type is not proven.
Small helper bitfield names such as u8_bits and x37_state_bits were used to model flag-byte access without overnaming every flag.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with multiple exact sizes, including PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(24), PAD_STACK(0x10), and PAD_STACK(40), to tune stack layout.
Used #pragma dont_inline on/reset and #pragma push/pop around functions such as fn_801855BC, fn_801884F8, and fn_801891F4 to manage MWCC inlining and scheduling.
Added inline/noInline variants such as fn_801884F8_inline and fn_8018846C_noInline so callers could reproduce expected inline behavior independently from the exported function body.
Modeled flag bytes as bitfields in lbl_804736C0.x36/x37 and u8_bits to improve branch/rlwimi-style codegen for animation and match-end flags.
Preserved duplicated scene setup blocks and explicit HSD call order in fn_80186634/gm_80187F48_OnEnter rather than factoring them, likely to match original scheduling and side effects.
Used explicit casts and unsigned comparisons, e.g. (s32), (u8), u16 checks, and U/ULL constants, to guide MWCC branch generation.
Represented mixed string/integer data with raw static arrays and casts, such as lbl_803D9750 entries and byte-array text constants, to keep data layout close to the target.
Documented remaining diffs as BSS/data/SDA relocation offsets, register swaps, prologue scheduling, or data-section base issues; this is useful triage for future link/layout work.
Moved from raw ptr[index] accesses to typed globals like lbl_80473700.result_cache and lbl_80473700.css while tracking that such cleanup can alter BSS offset diffs.
review_feedback: No human GitHub review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code 59.50% (+0.05%, +1932 bytes) for GALE01, with 5 new matches and 30 improvements in unmatched gm_1832 items.
decomp.dev reported one broken match: main/melee/gm/gm_1832 fn_801884F8 changed from 100.00% to 99.82%, plus a .bss unmatched regression.
The author commented: "Lots of fuzzy matches, not a lot of 100% ones, I still believe this is an improvement :)"
The author also commented that the broken function was "data / offset stuff" and "will be resolved when linking".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve fn_801884F8 or lbl_80473700 BSS/data offset regressions after linking/layout changes.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of gm_1832 address-named functions, especially Classic intro, VS intro, and Training Mode helpers.
Search for follow-up validation or resizing of TrainingModeState, CssSubStruct, lbl_804735E8, and lbl_804736C0 layouts.
Search for other PRs using bitfields to match rlwimi/codegen in game-mode state bytes.
Search for PRs that finish fuzzy gm_1832 matches listed here, especially fn_8018325C, fn_80184138, fn_801891F4, fn_80186634, gm_80187F48_OnEnter, and gm_80189CDC.
